debt_collection.name: Debt Collection / Bank Recovery Notice
debt_collection.governing_law: CBN Consumer Protection Framework 2016, FCCPC Act 2022, Banks and Other Financial Institutions Act
key_rights: 21 days to respond to a formal demand notice before legal action
Right to request a full statement of account from the bank
CBN Consumer Protection Framework prohibits harassment and false statements
Right to file complaint with CBN Consumer Protection Department — free
Right to complain to FCCPC (Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission)
Debt collectors cannot threaten imprisonment for civil debt under Nigerian law
common_violations: Threatening criminal prosecution or arrest for civil bank debt
Recovery agents using harassment, threats, or visiting family members
Not providing adequate notice before asset recovery action
Adding undisclosed charges or misrepresenting amount owed
Contacting employer or publicizing debt without court order
debt_collection.forum: CBN Consumer Protection Department or Federal High Court
debt_collection.template_response: I write in response to your notice dated [DATE] regarding account [NUMBER]. I request a complete and certified statement of account showing all principal, interest, and charges. I am aware of my rights under the CBN Consumer Protection Framework and the FCCPC Act 2022. I dispute the amount claimed and require proper documentation. I will approach the CBN Consumer Protection Department if this matter is not addressed appropriately.

eviction_notice.name: Eviction / Quit Notice
eviction_notice.governing_law: Recovery of Premises Act, Rent Control and Recovery of Residential Premises Laws (state specific)
key_rights: Monthly tenancy: 7 days minimum notice to quit
Annual tenancy: 6 months minimum notice to quit
Landlord must obtain court order before forcible eviction
Right to challenge notice validity in court
Cannot be removed by landlord without valid court order and bailiff
common_violations: Forcible eviction without obtaining a court order (criminal offence)
Removing tenant belongings without court order
Cutting off electricity, water, or access to force vacation
Insufficient notice period for the type of tenancy
eviction_notice.forum: Magistrate Court (Rent Division) or State High Court
eviction_notice.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of your quit notice dated [DATE]. I contest the validity of this notice on the grounds that [insufficient notice period / notice not properly served]. I am aware that you cannot effect my eviction without a court order from the Magistrate Court. I request that you withdraw this notice or proceed through proper legal channels.

court_summons.name: Court Summons / Writ of Summons
court_summons.governing_law: Sheriffs and Civil Process Act, Federal High Court Civil Procedure Rules, State High Court Rules
key_rights: 30 days to enter appearance and file defence in Federal High Court
Failure to respond results in judgment in default
Right to legal representation
Right to challenge improper service of process
Right to free legal aid through Legal Aid Council of Nigeria
common_violations: Summons not properly served per Sheriffs and Civil Process Act
Wrong court jurisdiction
Insufficient particulars of claim
court_summons.forum: The issuing court (Federal or State High Court)
court_summons.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the Writ of Summons in Suit No. [NUMBER]. I intend to enter appearance and file my Statement of Defence within the time prescribed. I dispute the claim and will respond fully through proper legal channels.

employment_termination.name: Termination / Retrenchment Letter
employment_termination.governing_law: Labour Act Cap L1 LFN 2004, National Industrial Court Act, Trade Disputes Act
key_rights: Minimum one month notice or one month pay in lieu for monthly paid employees
Right to challenge wrongful termination at National Industrial Court
Entitlement to accrued leave allowance and outstanding wages at termination
Trade union members have additional protections against anti-union dismissal
Right to severance pay if redundancy is genuine and procedurally correct
common_violations: Termination without minimum notice or pay in lieu under Labour Act
Non-payment of accrued wages, leave allowance, or gratuity
Termination for trade union activity or protected whistleblowing
Constructive dismissal through unbearable working conditions
employment_termination.template_response: I acknowledge my termination as communicated on [DATE]. Under the Labour Act Cap L1, I am entitled to one month notice or pay in lieu. I request immediate payment of all outstanding wages, accrued leave allowance, and any gratuity owed. I reserve my right to approach the National Industrial Court if my entitlements are not settled within 14 days.

consumer_notice.name: Consumer / Company Notice
consumer_notice.governing_law: Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act 2018 (FCCPA), FCCPC Act 2022
key_rights: Right to file complaint with FCCPC — Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission
Right to fair hearing and redress for defective goods and services
Companies must provide clear warranty terms and honor them
Right to seek compensation for damage caused by defective products
FCCPC Consumer Protection Helpline: 0700-FCCPC-NG
common_violations: False or deceptive representation of goods or services
Failure to honor warranty claims
Charging for services not rendered
Unconscionable conduct in consumer transactions
consumer_notice.template_response: I write to formally notify you of a consumer complaint under the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act 2018. I purchased [product/service] on [date] for [amount]. The [product/service] fails to conform to what was represented and is defective. I demand [refund/replacement/repair] within 14 days. Failing resolution, I will file a formal complaint with the FCCPC.
